next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 16

Two days after Sable walked out of the farmhouse, the three of them were sleeping in an abandoned Hoarded waystation east of the direct road to the Scour. Vel had taken the long loop through old irrigation cuts and relay posts because the straight southern line was open ground, easy to watch, and probably held by people with better levels than theirs if Sable was right. The loop bought them cover, one intact pump, three walls still standing, and about two days before the basin.

The road had cost them anyway. Kem spent seven levels in the stupid little bites that passed for caution when you still had levels left: one for light in a collapsed culvert, two bracing a washout while Dren crossed with his left arm pinned to his side, one forcing water through a pump handle rusted half shut, three more in scraps of strength and reinforcement when Margins infrastructure gave out under their feet. Vel spent two of her own, one on concealment when a dust line to the north moved too evenly to be weather, one keeping the waystation stairs from coming down under Dren's weight. By dusk Kem was at eighteen and she was at fourteen.

Dren was packing before sunrise.

Vel heard him through the thin wall of the waystation, a bedroll tied wrong, retied, tied wrong again. A buckle knocked stone. A strap dragged over wood. A muffled curse when leather caught on stone. She knew what she was listening to. She'd been expecting it since the farmhouse, since Sable's warning, since Dren had started looking south with the face of a man doing sums against a child's pain.

Outside, the dawn...
open_hooks_to_carry: Sable alone at 238, having dismissed her team; six weeks of transformation
Sable's encounter with a former Hoarded defector carrying Stillness damage — speech slowing, eyes drifting, the condition portable and visible outside the Vault
Three-way confrontation in a Margins farmhouse — Sable arrives to a broken alliance; dark comedy of bureaucratic speech patterns meeting raw reality
Kem tells Sable about Lord Aldraste's Stillness; Sable recognizes it from the defector she just met
Sable's choice: she cannot return him to what she now understands as slow death
Red Reserve has a permanent Scour posting — classified assignment, agents stationed for years — the Hoarded have been guarding the threshold longer than Vel realized
Sable heads south, not back to the Vault — driven by a personal question: if zero is a door, then everything she served was protecting people from freedom

payoffs_to_land.description: Stillness encountered through both Kem's testimony and the Margins defector — Sable recognizes it from Council members she's worked with for decades, and the double confirmation breaks her institutional loyalty
The Red Reserve deployment is both new (Council authorization for Kem retrieval) and old (permanent Scour garrison) — the escalation reframes the Hoarded as active suppressors

prev_tail_excerpt:
...North still existed. The quarryman had offered them solid ground, maybe work in the morning. Twenty-five levels spent sparingly could buy Kem years. He could turn around, disappear into some Spent town, learn to mend roofs or unload carts, spend one point a year and call it wisdom.

Instead he watched Vel mark a route through broken ground with sixteen levels left and a hand that had not entirely stopped shaking since the house wall. He tried, for a second, to picture himself taking the north road at dawn. The picture would not hold. It kept breaking on the house, the joist under his palm, the girl's hands on her mother's face.

Kem got up and crossed to the fire.

"Where does her writing stop?"

Vel looked at him for the first time since the rescue. She understood the question. After a beat she touched the page. "Two, maybe three days south. Depending on the ground."

He looked at the mark, then at the lines after it in Vel's tighter hand.

"And tomorrow?"

"Bad footing till noon. Better after the ridge."

A nod did for agreement. He handed the journal back, went to his bedroll, tightened the straps on his pack, and set it within reach of his hand for the dark.

Dren was already asleep, or doing a fair imitation. Somewhere lower in the settlement a child sang to herself while pots were scraped clean. Firewood snapped. Vel turned one page, then another.

Kem lay down without taking the signet back out. Anger was still there. So was the unanswered damage between them. Morning would not repair either one.

His pack waited beside him, buckled for south.

They would walk south.
secondary_character_beats: Sable's decision is a personal identity crisis, not a heroic defection — she is not joining anyone, she is failing in a way she cannot make herself regret
The Margins defector with Stillness damage makes the condition visceral and present-tense — not backstory but evidence

setups_to_plant.description: Sable's personal question about zero — if the threshold is real, the entire Hoarded philosophy was suppressing freedom — drives her south and toward the Scour, seeding her essential contribution in Ch 18
The Red Reserve's permanent Scour posting reveals the Hoarded have been suppressing threshold knowledge for years — the garrison knows the terrain and the mechanism

state_deltas_required: Sable's identity crisis meeting the fractured alliance; the revelation of the Hoarded's active threshold suppression; present-tense Stillness through the Margins defector

Sable becomes a person rather than a force; the Hoarded revealed as active suppressors of the threshold; Sable's personal question drives her south; the Red Reserve threat activates with the permanent Scour posting
